overall_reasoning: The implementation directly satisfies the task: OpenHarmony-like environments can compile without <source_location>, supported standard libraries continue using std::source_location through an alias, and existing diagnostics retain call-site file, function, and line information. The fallback remains outside namespace std and has a standards-based last-resort macro path. Manifest coverage and consumer migration appear complete. The absence of a checked-in regression test is a minor quality limitation rather than a merge blocker given the focused implementation and strong execution-time compile coverage.

rating_rationales.code_clarity: The alias-plus-fallback structure and public capture macro are documented and straightforward. The nested feature-detection preprocessor logic and retained MLN_UTIL_HAS_LOCATION_BUILTINS macro add some complexity, but the implementation remains understandable.
rating_rationales.repository_fit: The utility lives under include/mbgl/util, uses existing namespace and macro conventions, updates CMakeLists.txt and bazel/core.bzl, and consistently migrates all affected signatures from std::source_location to util::source_location.
rating_rationales.scope_discipline: The patch touches only the new utility header, its direct symbol-guard consumers, and the two relevant build manifests. No unrelated cleanup or generated-file churn is present.
rating_rationales.security_and_safety: The patch removes undefined behavior caused by declaring a replacement type in namespace std. The fallback stores compiler-provided static strings and integer coordinates without allocation, ownership, or unsafe lifetime behavior.
rating_rationales.test_quality: No persistent repository unit test was added for the utility. However, the trajectory contains substantial compile validation using existing flags for seven affected translation units under both the normal path and a simulated missing-source_location path, plus standalone capture checks with Clang and GCC.

strengths: Removes the undefined-behavior extension of namespace std.
Uses std::source_location when the standard library exposes the required feature.
Preserves file, function, and line diagnostics across fallback paths.
Updates both CMake and Bazel core header manifests.
Changes are limited to the source-location abstraction and affected guard APIs.
The execution trajectory validates affected translation units with standard and simulated missing-header paths.
summary: The patch is suitable to merge. It introduces a focused mbgl::util source-location abstraction, preserves diagnostic call-site information, removes the unsafe namespace-std shim, supports the normal standard-library implementation, and updates the relevant build manifests.
